text.title: Code Quality Trends Core Rules
text.summary: 5 hard rules for a useful quality audit.

rule.statement: Audit is re-run every 90 days; reports older than 120 days are flagged stale on read.

rationale.source: TC39 yearly cadence; tsconfig releases; ESLint major releases

rule.rationale: Best-practice flags rotate; a stale audit gives false confidence.

rule.statement: TypeScript: strict=true is the floor; Python: mypy --strict OR pyright strict; agent may not relax these without owner approval.

rationale.source: TypeScript docs — strict; mypy docs — strict mode

rule.rationale: Strict mode catches real bugs at compile time; relaxing it migrates the bug to runtime.

rule.statement: Remediation is shipped as a config diff, never as a whole-file overwrite.
rule.rationale: Whole-file replacement loses local customisations and project-specific exclusions.

rule.statement: Frontend audits include a Lighthouse / Web-Vitals budget; backend audits include a p95 latency budget per endpoint.

rationale.source: web.dev Core Web Vitals; Google SRE — latency budgets

rule.rationale: Performance regressions slip when no budget exists to detect them; a quality audit without perf is incomplete.

rule.statement: Audit runs CodeQL / Semgrep / pip-audit / npm-audit and reports new high-severity findings in the report's security section.
rule.rationale: Security regressions ride along with config drift; same audit cycle catches both.
